# Wave H7 — Ingest hygiene + visual polish

## Lessons baked in (from H6 retro)
- Agents must `git add` only owned paths, not `-A` (Yoda's commit accidentally absorbed Leia's files).
- 87 cross-team dups surfaced means latent data drift — bake detection into the pipeline so it stops accumulating.
- `pgrep` guard now in place — orchestrator runs `--apply` only after killing dev servers (verified working H6).

## User request context
H6 retro proposed H7 candidates. Skipping user-blocked queues (laxnumbers/score-reconcile) and snapshot:db CRON (needs Azure Storage secret). Picking three independent lanes that need no user input.

## Lanes

| Lane | Fleet | Effort | Scope | Blocked by |
|------|-------|--------|-------|------------|
| 1 | Han 😉🚀 | M | Ingest emits composite + cross-team-dup warnings to `ingest_anomalies` | — |
| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | M | Leaders-table inline sparklines (top-N per metric) | — |
| 3 | Leia 👑💁‍♀️ | S | Team detail page: W/L badges per game + GF/GA chart | — |

## Lane scope contracts (locked)

### Lane 1 (Han) — Ingest hygiene warnings
- EDIT `packages/ingest/src/crawler.ts` (or wherever player rows are written; look for the player-stat insert path used by `parsePlayerStatLine` / quarter parser):
  - When extracted player name matches `/(.+?)\s+(?:and|&)\s+(.+)/i` → **don't** create the composite row; log to `ingest_anomalies` with `strategy_attempted='composite-name-detected'`, `reason='Composite name "X and Y" not split during ingest; manual review required'`.
  - The split tooling already exists (`split:composite-players`); this stops new composites at the source.
- NEW `packages/ingest/src/scripts/detectCrossTeamDups.ts` — read-only audit; finds players with same `LOWER(TRIM(name))` on multiple `team_id`. Emits each as `ingest_anomalies` row with `strategy_attempted='cross-team-duplicate-name'`. Idempotent (skip if already present for same name+team-pair). Run via `pnpm --filter @pll/ingest detect:cross-team-dups` (no --apply needed; this script always writes anomalies, never mutates players).
- Tests for both behaviors (in-memory DB).
- Add npm script `detect:cross-team-dups`.

### Lane 2 (Yoda) — Leaders-table sparklines
- NEW `packages/server/src/routes/leaderSparklines.ts` (or extend `leaders.ts`) — endpoint `GET /api/leaders/players/sparklines?metric=goals&limit=10` returns array of `{ player_id, name, perGame: number[] }` for top-N players sorted by total of that metric.
- EDIT `packages/web/src/views/leaders.ts` — fetch sparklines after the table renders; render an inline 80×24 canvas sparkline per row (line chart of per-game values). Hidden gracefully if API fails or empty.
- NEW `packages/web/src/charts/sparkline.ts` — small reusable canvas function `drawSparkline(canvas, values, opts?)`.
- Tests: server route unit test (3+ players, correct shape); web util test for sparkline draw (jsdom canvas mock or pure math test of normalization).

### Lane 3 (Leia) — Team detail enhancements
- EDIT `packages/web/src/views/teamDetail.ts` — under the schedule table:
  - Add a W/L badge column (✅ W / ❌ L / ⚪ T or pending) computed from `team_score` vs `opponent_score` for completed games.
  - Add a small GF/GA per-game chart (use existing `renderPerGameTrend` or a fresh helper if it doesn't fit) plotted from the schedule.
- Boundaries: don't touch leaders or playerDetail; don't touch the server route (use existing `/api/teams/:id`).
- Tests: jsdom render test asserting a W badge appears for a winning game and L for losing game.

## Wave H7 outcomes

| Lane | Fleet | Outcome |
|------|-------|---------|
| 2 | Yoda 👽✨ | ✅ Inline sparklines on `/leaders` players table. New `GET /api/leaders/players/sparklines?metric=&limit=` endpoint (whitelist: points/goals/assists/groundBalls/causedTurnovers/saves/faceoffWins; default limit=10, max=25). Web table now appends a `Trend` column with 80×24 canvas sparklines for the top 10 rows of the active metric (skips fo_pct & points_per_game). New `drawSparkline()` + `normalizeForSparkline()` helpers in `packages/web/src/charts/sparkline.ts`. Server: 5 new tests; Web: 9 new tests. `pnpm -r typecheck` ✅, server tests 121✅, web tests 53✅. |
| 3 | Leia 👑💁‍♀️ | ✅ Team detail page now shows pill-style W/L/T/pending result badges (✅ W / ❌ L / ⚪ T / —) per game and a canvas-based GF/GA "Season scoring trend" chart (hidden when 0 completed games). New helper `renderTeamScoreTrend` in `packages/web/src/charts/teamScoreTrend.ts` (canvas — `renderPerGameTrend` was single-series so a fresh helper kept blast radius small). 10 unit tests added covering badge mapping (W/L/T/pending/missing scores) and trend extraction (skip postponed, GF/GA from team perspective, chronological sort). `pnpm -r typecheck` clean, `pnpm --filter @pll/web test` 44/44 green. |
| 1 | Han 😉🚀 | ✅ Composite + cross-team-dup detection baked into ingest. `summaries.ts` now matches `/^(.+?)\s+(?:and|&)\s+(.+)$/i` against player names BEFORE upsert; on hit, skips the row and emits `composite-name-detected` anomaly with reason pointing to `split:composite-players`. New `packages/ingest/src/scripts/detectCrossTeamDups.ts` (always-writes audit, no `--apply` gate, idempotent via existence check on `strategy_attempted+raw_line`) — wired as `pnpm --filter @pll/ingest detect:cross-team-dups`. New `ParserStrategy` values: `composite-name-detected`, `cross-team-duplicate-name`. Tests: 3 for cross-team script (insert / idempotency / no-dups) + 1 in `summaries.test.ts` for composite skip+anomaly. `pnpm -r typecheck` ✅; `pnpm --filter @pll/ingest test` 384✅; manual `detect:cross-team-dups` against post-H6 DB → `groups found: 0` (clean). |

## Wave H7 retro

- ✅ All 3 lanes shipped clean. Zero cross-lane file leakage this wave (explicit `git add` paths fixed the H6 problem).
- ✅ `pgrep` guard from H6 caused zero friction (Han didn't need it; Yoda/Leia don't mutate DB).
- ✅ Pipeline-level detection (Lane 1) means future composite/dup drift gets surfaced into `/api/anomalies` and the data-quality view automatically — no more silent accumulation.
- 🔍 Live API smoke: `/api/leaders/players/sparklines?metric=goals&limit=3` returned 13/12/12-game arrays for Kostack/Carberry/Gallagher. Sparkline column rendering via tsx watch hot-reload — no manual restart needed.
- 📊 Tests delta this wave: +5 server, +19 web, +4 ingest. Totals: 121 / 53 / 384 — all green.
- 💡 Yoda's choice to gracefully degrade the sparkline column (logged warn + empty cells if API fails) is a nice pattern; worth replicating on team-detail chart and other chart additions.

## Next Wave proposal (H8)

### H8 candidate slate
- **[P0] Compare view** (M) — let user pick 2 players or 2 teams and see side-by-side stats + per-game trends. Highest visible-feature value; data already in API.
- **[P1] Mobile polish** (M) — leaders/team-detail tables overflow horizontally on small screens; needs responsive-table wrapper or column-priority hiding.
- **[P1] /anomalies UX** (S) — Han's pipeline now adds rows there; group/filter UI improvements (filter by `strategy_attempted`, mark-resolved button via PATCH).
- **[P2] LaxNumbers team aliases wire-in** (S) — laxnumbers CSV (team-name aliases for the LaxNumbers.com gap-fill source) is sitting at 102 unresolved rows; even a partial wire-in would unlock more gap-fill matches. **User-blocked: needs decisions on the CSV.**
- **[P2] Snapshot:db CRON** (S) — already scripted; needs `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` secret. **User-blocked.**

### Suggested H8 wave (3 lanes, balanced M/M/S)
- Lane 1 (M) — Han: compare-players view + new `/api/compare/players?ids=` endpoint
- Lane 2 (M) — Yoda: responsive-table wrapper applied to leaders + team-detail + dashboard
- Lane 3 (S) — Leia: anomalies view filter + mark-resolved button (read-only at first; resolve button can be follow-up if PATCH route adds scope)

### Decision: Ready for Wave H8?
- Recommend: **Yes** — none of the chosen lanes are user-blocked.
- User input still wanted (whenever convenient) for: laxnumbers CSV decisions + Azure Storage secret to unblock the [P2] items above.
